text:
i graded 207 MCP servers. the best one has 1 tool.

Google Colab MCP: execute_code. that's it. A+ (97.6/100). 88 tokens.

GitHub official: 80 tools. 15,927 tokens. F.

Notion official: 22 tools. 4,463 tokens. F.

more isn't better.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

the design philosophy behind Google's Colab MCP:

1 tool: execute_code(code: str). the LLM writes Python instead of calling 80 specific endpoints.

that's not a limitation. it's the whole point.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

Colab MCP vs Notion MCP:

same protocol. 48x more tokens. opposite grades.

1 tool vs 22 tools. A+ vs F.

if you're building an MCP server — what's your execute_code equivalent?

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html
